review_text: Lovely clean room and parks. Great staff!

Good location, short walk to the shops and town centre. Dog friendly cabin was basic and could use an upgrade. Staff were friendly and the kids enjoyed the facilities on offer. We all enjoyed all the wildlife roaming around the park.

We stayed 5 nights at an ensuite site.  The park on arrival looked nice with the grounds well maintained.  Reception staff were helpful making  check in a breeze.  But.. The amenities were rather lacklustre.  During our stay The acsessable toilet/family bathroom, 2 of the hand dryers in the male toilet/shower block, the boiling hot water dispenser in the camp kitchen and one of the washing machines in the laundry were all out of order for our entire stay. Thank goodness it wasn’t busy during our holiday.  If your staying in an ensuite site bring extra toilet paper. management only supply a “starter pack” after that’s gone, you supply your own.  I wouldn’t recommend staying here due to the lack of maintenance to the  facilities.
Best clean cabins ever.

Great location close to town and shops but far enough away to be quiet. Some facilities weren't working when we were there (hand driers in amenities and boiling water in kitchen) but was good even without these. Stayed in a powered site on the southern side  which was nice and quiet.

DO NOT STAY HERE!
Unless of course you want to be surrounded by elderly citizens who harass children. This park is not family friendly and supports older men abusing your children who are playing on the grassed areas. Which are not safe nor fenced off, with big concrete drains exposed.
The rooms are not as pictures depict they are tiny and under equipped, this may be due to majority of the staff members sitting in the back cabins smoking W***, filling caravans with the smell.
There is one lady in reception who was nice upon arrival. Other than that the other lady should maybe join the rest of her team down in the rear cabins. She could do with some R&R, you'd expect some kind of decency and to be spoken to as though you are human as you are paying extravagant prices for seriously low quality customer service.
We did not see many other children within the park, it seems as though others have learnt the same lesson in the past.

If the park would like their clientele to be geriatric, they should clearly state that in their website or during their booking process..

Rooms are very nice spacious  but our shower was dirty . We didn't have any internet whilst we stayed  and clearly it sayes wifi onthere ads and we were there for 5 nights. Grounds were very nice but  during dark there was no lighting  where we were outside

We stayed for four nights as a family of five in a two-bedroom Hideaway cabin, and unfortunately the experience was very disappointing.

The cabin itself was extremely small, even by cabin standards. On arrival, the floors were wet and still very dirty — so much so that several pairs of socks had to be thrown away after walking around inside. This was unacceptable and made the cabin feel unhygienic from the start. The bunk beds were old, the fridge was rusty, and storing food in it did not feel hygienic.

On arrival, we were initially allocated the incorrect cabin — one that slept four people instead of five, despite what had been booked and paid for. To be fair, the hotel manager did come and rectify this and provided the correct cabin. However, this situation, combined with other issues, did leave a poor impression. Prior to arrival, when speaking to reception staff on the phone due to delays caused by road closures travelling from Victoria into New South Wales, the staff member was very rude and unaccommodating. While keys were left out for late arrival, the interaction itself was disappointing.

We were also staying in the park with friends and requested to be located closer together so the children could play. Despite asking about availability and flexibility, we were told there was absolutely no way this could be accommodated. This was frustrating, particularly as cabins directly next to our friends were visibly available for almost the entirety of our four-day stay.

The Wi-Fi was another major issue. Although a Wi-Fi code was provided, the internet dropped out constantly. Because it rained for much of the stay and there was not much to do within the park, reliance on the Wi-Fi was unavoidable and resulted in most personal mobile data being used within the first two days.

The indoor pool was advertised as heated but was actually quite cold. The spa was also not at spa temperature and felt almost as cold as the pool, making it uncomfortable to use.

The park itself feels quite old and tired, with limited activities for children. On top of the already high cost of staying as a family of five, it was disappointing that the games room required additional payment just to play air hockey and other games. Given the current cost-of-living pressures, this felt unreasonable.

The one standout positive was a staff member with an Irish accent who was responsible for cleaning and maintaining the cabins. She was absolutely delightful — friendly, helpful, and genuinely accommodating. She assisted with towels and was easily the highlight of the entire stay. Unfortunately, this level of service was not reflected in the reception or management staff, who were generally unhelpful.

Overall, even if the park itself remained the same, improved cleanliness, service, and flexibility would have made a significant difference. Based on this experience, it is difficult to recommend this park to families seeking value for money, basic cleanliness, and supportive customer service.

We stayed in the 2BR villa which was discusting and dirty. The “villa” is tiny, absolutely no space to move. The table was broken, the microwave was dirty and started smoking when we tried to use it.
Nowhere to sit outside either.
I have never left a review and we have stayed in a lot of caravan parks, but I really felt like I had to do this review.
Zero

We stayed here for two nights in a Polynesian cabin as a stopover on our trip up the East Coast. We called to asked if the room was ready early as it was pouring rain and the lady on the phone was terribly rude.
Not a great first impression. She mentioned that she would call when it was ready. She never called so we rocked up 45 minutes after check in time, after finding somewhere to have lunch to kill the time. After asking if the room was ready, the lady replied with, ‘I don’t think so, oh wait it is.’
We arrived at the cabin and all the floors were still soaking wet after being mopped. Kids were so excited for the hot spa and heated pool and upon check in we were told that the pool was heated to 29 degrees and the spa to 32 degrees. We eagerly unpacked our bags and got our swimmers on, both the pool and spa were cold! It was very disappointing after paying over $400 a night and the one facility (the reason we booked here), that we could use in the rain, was cold.

Other things:
- the spa was green the first day we were here, better the second.
- the curtains do not block out light from other peoples cabins= we ended up putting a blanket over the rail so that we could make it darker to sleep.

I wouldn’t pay this much to stay here again!
Good caravan park lots of stuff for the kids to do, only negative was there was lots of rain so there were so limits on outdoor activities.

Not worth the money, we needed to stay here for one night and cost a fortune for one night, $170 for a night but the next night was $119.. can’t work out why. The facilities are okay! Not the best but not the worst, but for $170 a night they would be 2/10 for me. Other sites for far cheaper with better facilities

This is quite a low budget option stay and this reflects in the premises. Everything is just tired. However sheets are clean.

Major downside was that I left my health ring behind which they found. I asked them to post it out to me, at my cost, which they did. The envelope arrived undamaged (luckily as it was simply in a paper envelope! You would have thought someone would have sent in something more secure!) uninsured or traceable despite me stating I’d cover all costs. I thought this totally flat envelope looked odd when it arrived so filmed us opening the envelop and guess what, expensive health ring wasn’t in there! The envelope was still completely sealed and undamaged as per the video shows. I sent the video to Merimbula who insist it was put in there. So how did it escape?

I also contacted them on the 19th and they said they’d post it out. By the 29th it hadn’t arrived so I followed up. The date stamp on the envelope states the 29th so I assume they’d forgotten to post it. It finally arrived on the 31st but as I’ve said, no ring!

Very disappointed and the ring is logged as stolen so now can’t be activated by anyone.

We stayed one night on an ensuite site. The campground itself is great, with plenty to do for children and a heated indoor swimming pool. The bathroom was modern, but not very clean upon our arrival. Towels in the ensuite would be a nice addition. The staff should pay more attention to cleaning before guests arrive. camp kitchen is well-equipped. The city center can be reached in about 15 minutes on foot.
Easy walk in to town. Very tidy park. Bell birds galore.

We stayed one night on ensuite site. Loved the indoor pool, though it could use a clean. The ensuite looked nearly new but was not clean, unfortunately. So much potential here, but you need to maintain your place. Lots of birds around and what they leave behind. It was not busy here right now. Just use the time you have to clean up, guys!
Beautiful, love the heated pool! Lots of kangaroos around, water hens and ducks!
Stayed last night , receptionist went out of her way for us even staying back 10 minutes after closing time to book us in , so clean and peaceful will definitely be back . Thank You ☺️
Thank you for your wonderful service we appreciate your kindness

Disappointed as a first time stay  wifi, advertised as free wifi - good luck so poor it doesn’t work at all inside the cabins, when we try to use it outside in front of the cabins/ balcony we have 2 bars at a town which has only 4G makes it very hard to do any work download emails and reports.  Have complained to the service desk they pretend that it will get fixed and then nothing changes and happens. The rooms are old style, shower is very very slippery squashed for a cabin that sleeps 6 people.

3 bed Cabin - spacious design. TV in family and master bedroom. 2 showers and toilets. Large full kitchen. Indoor heated pool and spa to die for. Awesome kids playground. Will definitely stay there again.
I'm stayying in a powered site. Lovely friendly park. Well looked after. Great indoor pool, spa, play equipment for the kids. It has it all.
Only one vehicle per villa..small rooms. AC outdoor unit near the rooms making noise, grumpy woman at the reception.😔

Kids liked the Pool and bouncing pillow.
One of the two bbq's wasn't working, the one that was was working was clean and worked well.
In the games room the table soccer game is dangerous for younger players as the metal rods had no end caps on them, this meant an eye poking hazard.
Our Cabin was run down and lacked maintenance. It wasn't clean. We were disappointed.

Great central location... park the car and ride/walk to town etc..
Park has all the amenities to keep the kids entertained and the heated pool is a winner!
Great place to stay friendly staff and awesome clean facility's. Plenty for the kids to do would recommend highly for the family holiday

Lovely tidy clean holiday park however in the week we were there it was more than half empty most of the time, however we were given a smallish site with big van uptight against a holiday cabin looking into their bedrooms, and over looking the flats, arguably the worst site in the park.☠️

Stayed 7 nights, 3 would have been better. Arrived to a grumpy woman behind the desk.  Clean & well maintained park. Playground needs more, jump pillow was small but effective, mini golf was dull no colour or fun things about it, pool was good although the kids run absolutely rampant in there and the toilets were disgusting one day. Kids riding their electric scooters were flying around the park with absolutely no enforcement. Our van site itself was a good size, ensuite was clean and tidy. Had some yobbo’s pull in behind us and decided to congregate around our bed end not in their allocated site which was just great. Coins to use laundry but couldn’t get money out of reception for it. Games room had a coin changer and that was broken so we had to get cash out of office which was laughable. Wildlife a treat although kangaroo poo everywhere.
Overall quite good experience although could do with improvements contacting reception when issues arise or cleaning needs to be done. Wild life was great for my older parents.
Good access to park. Very nice layout. Sites are good size. Lots of wildlife

Understanding that we only paid $119 per night but we didn’t stay for the 2nd night we booked. The cabin was called a cottage and was very rundown and dirty. Paint chipped everywhere, hairs, cobwebs. Felt dirty. Ended up leaving and driving home. The caravan sites looked ok and the amenities block was better than our cabin

Very nice place for a holiday. The pool is heated so it’s still good in winter. Jumping pillow is bouncy. Mini Golf is a fun challenge. And the games room is fun. But be careful if you’re bringing kids because this young kid started climbing a mountain that’s really tall and connected to the place. It was very steep and dangerous.

Absolutely wonderful.  Noelene at the office went above and beyond with her customer service the whole time we were there.  Because of her we will definitely return to this park.  The facilities there were also fabulous, well looked after and very clean.  The park was also very close to the shopping area, within walking distance which was very convenient.  The reception you get when you arrive at a park is very important to your view of the park and your enjoyment whilst there.  Thank you Noelene for your wonderful service whilst we were there.
A great place for a caravan, handy to the city.
Lovely park ,Clean well kept amenities & grounds . very helpful staff . See you again soon .

We really loved our stay here, we stayed in a bungalow, me and my dog. The owner was lovely and made us feel very welcome. Close location to wollies and walking distance to Main Street. Really appreciated having everything in the bungalow to cook, and chill out. Reverse cycle kept it nice and warm too. Will stay again.
Totally enjoyed our stay, kids had a awesome time in heated pool and basketball courts. The games room was ok
Awesome park for anyone. Love our cabin. Close to town. We walked in a couple of times. Will go again.

We had 3 nights here we had an ensuite site it was spotless
Place was beautiful well kept
Lady in office was lovely
Great pool games room and huge camp kitchen
Would definitely stay here again

Parked at the correct position out side reception to check in & an employee reversed one of the parks golf carts into the side of our new motorhome! causing $2000 worth of damage. 2 months later & a lot of running around the motorhome has been repaired & paid for by the park. We were also allotted a way to small a site despite informing the park the size of our motorhome. Our motorhomes slide out was 500mm away from the caravan next door causing a row between nieghbours, when discussed with the parks manager we were then allotted a more suitable site. Worst park we have visited, dont bother, there are better in Merimbula.
Super friendly accomadating staff. Such a lovely property and great location!
Great holiday place
Close to town centre and easy access to all amenities. Park is nice and well maintained.

Do not book villa 4 is extremely old, couch is old and dirty, beds old and rusted, bathroom sink peeling off, kettle full of limescale, dining table for 4 people with only 3 chairs plus the price is ridiculous. This is not a villa is an extremely old and overused caravan. Better off camping on the grass!

Our Hotel room was terrible. Sand on the floor and hairs in bathroom.|The blind over the kitchen sink was filthy. Curtains were hanging off.|Very disappointed ☹️  We didn’t have the energy to complain and didn’t want to spoil our time. We will never recommend.

Stayed at the motel, very clean and simple
Room includes mid-size fridge and microwave. Located just outside of Merimbula town centre

Booked in over Easter for a weeks (school holidays) from the minute I walked in the door of our villa I wanted to leave. We ended up staying 3 nights only for the sake of the kids. The villa was a total disgrace. Over $300 dollar a night (give or take some days because of Easter p/h). See pics. Everything in the villa is super old, over used, falling in pieces. Mismatching dining chairs and missing one chair. What a misery! Rusted bunk beds, the bedroom you can’t even fit to walk from either side of the bed. The bathroom sink peeling off, the kettle full of limescale. The couch dirty and super old looks like they pick up the furniture from the street. Would never ever go back there. Last there are ticks and my partner got bitten. The villas are a NO NO don’t believe the pictures these people are deceivers. Disgusting villa. Awful experience. Old, dirty, super tiny. -1 stars
Lovely caravan site. Pool was to die for, loved it! Extremely clean everywhere on the park. We’ll run and super friendly
The Tasman is good for families we stayed for a week in a cabin the kids had fun
Enjoyed the stay and facilities. The pool and spa is very well maintained and the rooms are very clean

Great location at the top of town close by to the sapphire club. Excellent facilities for caravans plenty of room on the sites lots of cabins to hire, heated swimming pool mini golf games room basketball court. Not over priced great camp kitchen with all the modern appliances TV in the room. WiFi excellent reception

Good service
Friendly staff
Dog Friendly
Close to services

Brilliant caravan park and. Highly recommend, love how close it was to the Main Street yet felt like I was in the country with the lovely surrounds. The pool was wonderful, toilets and laundry were spotless, staff were excellent. We would definitely recommend and be visiting again
Didn't rate rooms as we had a powered ensuite site. Fantastic place to stay love the heated pool ❤️ will definitely be back again
Older cabins. For the price paid comparing what we have stayed at, this was overpriced. However for caravans would be very nice. Heated under cover pool, children's playground. Mini golf. Games room.

Although our Ensuite Site was good, the pricing was appalling. We were charged $99 per night (stayed 10 nights) while the people behind us who had exactly the same site were charged $55 per night. After two emails to head office and multiple phone calls, i finally rang the park direct to question the $44 a night price difference and was told that they decided to have a sale at the start of February. $10-$20 a night decrease i think is fair, but $44 a night!!!!!!! What a joke! We were not the only people there questioning the different pricing. The park owners/managers really need to know that people do talk amongst themselves when staying in caravan parks!

Absolutely lovely place,  plenty of room, extremely quiet and so lovely and green. Well maintained, clean, and all the amenities you could need. The wildlife is great nightly kangaroos rabbits and cute lizards always up for a feed of fruit. Pool and spa are very clean. Would certainly recommend to anyone wantonness a cabin or caravan site for a holiday like we have. Just 1 Min from the main city centre and great restaurants for meals. Aldi and woollies are also great if you want to stay in. Cabins are very stylish spacious and clean. Reasonable prices, too. Love our stay very much.

Very disappointing visit this year, we booked in November for February stay, were a bit shocked at price, but stupidly believed this was the rate for ALL power sites....alas after talking to many fellow caravaners, we soon realised there were many different prices charged. We tried, in vain, to speak with management but the only response was a generic email....also park has gone down hill, toilets often not very clean, lawns a bit overgrown. So my message is be very careful of price if calling direct, because the price is much higher than on Tasman website...so not sure if we were scammed....beware.

Amazing location
Our cabin was clean and well taken care of
We stayed for 7 nights and had a blast
Grounds were always clean and plenty of wildlife around
Many activities for the children and very safe for them to run around
Would definitely recommend

Stayed for 2 nights- pathetic condition of rooms. Hair everywhere- on bed, sofa, cushions. Bunk beds were as bad- very dirty and it dint look like somebody even bothered to change after previous people were gone. Second day, there were ants everywhere in kitchen with empty kitchen slab. Staff mentioned as it rained, it happens. The pan in kitchen was so bad and had no Teflon coating left on it. Staff did not see any issue with it. Never ever booking it again. Location was okay though.

We stayed in this park ten years ago before it was a Tasman and unfortunately, it has been upgraded, renovated or added to since that time.|We were booked in a cabin and told we were ‘upgraded’ . We were very disappointed that the upgraded cabin was smaller than the one we had originally booked|When we walked in, there were cobwebs in the bathroom and toilet, the doors and walls were chipped , Cleary no care about presentation.|The water went off twice when we were there.|Can’t play mini golf after 5pm - which is ridiculous in the summer|Months.|The bbq has been taken off our balcony, no replacement.|Office staff seem ‘over it’. Such a shame cause it’s a great location  .|Would def stay at NRMA Merimbula instead

Great location but better hope it doesn’t rain , multiple sites under water poor damage eg road higher than sites roads became a river and sites fill up with water , the use of disinfectant is highly suggested for toilets , Wifi it’s for the bin regardless when the park was full or empty , hot water not working for days , power outages, blocked dumping point , washing machine only take coins ,I normally don’t write reviews but if your charging top end rates there’s a bit of work to be done to justify your prices not including the price increase coming…….

The staff was excellent. Stayed for 8 nights. Kids had a ball. The pool and toilets were cleaned every day. The only downside was the hot water being repaired for the duration of our stay. The great thing was we had text notifications of the repair times which was great.

Friendly and hospitable staff at the Park. They did their best to make sure our stay was as comfortable and enjoyable as possible. Unfortunately there is so much they can do with substandard facilities. Blocked toilets and dump points, power outages in parts of the park, poorly designed streets and camp sites that lack any sort of basic drainage causing flooding on camp sites. Friends had to dig a trench leading to the street to run the water away from their tent and annexe. At $131 per night plus added cost for kids and a pending increase on the base rate of $30 for 2025, let’s be real. This park is leading the race as the most expensive park in the area and a comfortable last in terms of quality. I guess Tasman aren’t too concerned even if occupancy dips to 50% at those rates. Lower wage costs, power and water utilisation down. There are always other victims searching for their next holiday experience. Feel for the management team on site.

A lovely family friendly park. Clean amenities. Fantastic camp kitchen. Playground, jumping pillow and indoor pool Great for kids. Also free mini golf which was a hit. Cricket/basketball facilities and safe for kids to run and scoot around. Always something on - they held a kids disco during our stay which was a highlight! Local vendors most days ie. Coffee vans and snow cone makers. Fabulous location, walking distance into merimbula and woolies across the road. They had an issue with hot water supply while we were there but they were actively trying to fix it, opened up an ensuite site to assist and kept communication up to all of us via text. You are immersed in mountains and kangaroos hopping around regularly.

We spent 17 days at this park - hot water problems for 5 of those day, communication was not kept up daily , they opened ONE ensuite for a completely packed out caravan park many many children and adults.  This park Is approx 120 per night give or take depending on dates and then add 10 per child per night- not even a voucher for an ice cream - nothing.  The bathrooms themselves were only cleaned once a day - ridiculous for the amount of people there.  Women’s cleaned pretty well- mens there was a small amount  of blood on a wall the entire time, the urinal stunk and there was a spit at eye level on the urinal the entire time-  the manager was tearing her hair out trying to t to hire portable bathrooms etc - Tasman themselves pretty big Australia wide - she seemed not to be supported- the washing machines take 6 one dollar coins - office cannot sell you the coins on card - ridiculous who carries cash around - just want to say the manager was good not wanting to criticise her.  The other major detail is the car sites, the rigs are huge these days and the cars that pull them oversized.  Many many were half on the road and not fully in their allotted site.  When  arriving or leaving you had to get the entire street almost to move cars, this should be policed and it was not - we had quests for two hours who had pets- they were very quick to get them out of the park by phone and then personally by buggy.  If only they policed the cars the same way- we left early and just got out of our spot with inches to spare, overall packed in like sardines- a bit disappointing.

We absolutely love this caravan park! The staff, especially Cynthia and her team, are incredibly friendly and always greet you with a warm smile. The sites are spacious and well-maintained, and the park's location is ideal - close to shops yet sheltered from the wind, unlike some other parks in the area. We've been loyal guests for over a decade and have cherished countless wonderful memories here. We look forward to many more visits in the future.

The worst accomodation ever wouldn’t stay there if it was the last resort and free ! It’s a disgrace what they charge compared to what they provide ! When we booked was promised something got the total opposite ! Staff a liars ! Should tell the truth never again 😡😡😡

Prime location while being secluded away from the main roads. 10min walk from the Mitre 10 Bus stop which goes to Bega and Tura Beach. Staff are very friendly and accommodating for my needs however reception closes at 5pm which means no late check-in.

Wi-Fi was non existent in Kalinda Cabin 3 due to the location but was very good once it was able to connect in the Camp Kitchen.

Room service happens once a week and whether they make your bed is purely dependant on who services your room. After staying in a 4 star hotel in outer suburbia Paris, the making of the beds situation isn't so bad especially when the beds are straightforward to make.

Spa is as warm as the indoor pool and the indoor pool is warm enough to be warmer than the ocean.

Worth it to stay here. Will come back when I return to Merimbula.

Giving this holiday park 5 stars in correlation to price we paid for 2 night cabin stay ($280). We thought this was value for money.
Rooms - air conditioned, clean, TV. A little small but for 2 adults and one baby it was fine. (Noting it would be a tight squeeze for a cot)
- facilities great for kids, jumping balloon was a hit, not a huge fan of an indoor pool in 34 degree hit but understand this would be a hit for the majority of the year.
- great location to walk to town

Our site up the back was nothing special and we were offered a change however we enjoyed the wildlife wandering the park. The park is close to town and is being stuck up the back was very quiet. Amenities work fine. Pet friendly and dump point on site. Would stay again

Very clean park , beautiful swimming pool heated and hot spa all under cover
Walking distance to shops and town
Only stayed one night with our caravan but really enjoyed it. Fantastic camp kitchen and lots of fun things for the kids. Friendly and helpful staff. Would definitely stay again.

Nice quiet park but very religious on the rules, awake before had to sign a piece of paper online, And then the day you could only check in from 2:00 to 5:00 so they'd know what time I would turn up but had to message twice just to make sure and then follow up message to make sure again, maybe they've had problems in the past without people turning up or people turning up at the wrong time ........I don't know but it's a bit annoying when you driving there and have to stop to message them to say you'll be there at a time , very quiet park though, bit of mold on the backs of the curtains but still nice place
Great price for a powered site and lots of amenities to keep kids amused. Pool was a hit. Bathrooms could do with an upgrade tho. Otherwise highly recommend

I booked directly with tasman holiday parks online, which was a total breeze. Selecting the right room/unit for us was easy and hassle free. They sent me plenty of details and notification emails leading up to our stay as well as an easy pre-checkin email that sped up actual check in process.
The room is clean and tidy, well stocked with a mini fridge, microwave, toaster, kettle, cups, plates and utensils. Perfect size for our family of 4 and so cheap (these days). We came here to take our son to magic mountain for his birthday and have had a wonderful time JUST at the holiday park with all the fun and free things to do! There's a jumping pillow, heated pool, playground, giant checkers set, games/arcade room, sports courts, laundry rooms etc. All within a very short walk from our unit. The main shopping centre is literally 2mins drive away and magic mountain is just up the road. Extremely convenient and beautiful location. Very happy with our stay here.
Very unique accommodation. Very clean. Loved the kangaroos feeding in the evening.  Great walk to the beach, and the coffee at the silver van at the beach was special. Cheers

Staff were great so helpful & friendly.Great cabin with a large deck fenced area backing onto bushland behind a really good idea for travellers with dogs or families.Down near the laundry is a great dog wash area as well.
Also has a car charger on site.

We spent 5 days at the park and enjoyed our time. It was very quiet. The pool was fantastic and the amenities were always clean. It was great having the indoor camp kitchen as we travelled with friends so it was great to have somewhere out of the night air.
Our only disappointment was we couldn't use their terrific looking camp firepit. We specifically booked here so we could enjoy a nightly fire. We even took along our own firewood. We found out there is only a fire organised by the Park on a weekend night for an hour if a camp person is available. Many other parks allow you to light your own fire so I don't know why this park is different. It was winter and no issues with fire bans.

Watch what powered site you get.Some have concrete paths and if you have a back door the  path isn’t long enough .I hate been put on a bad site when their are plenty of other sites in  winter with paying $49 a night.Holes in the grassed areas also.I need to be careful

Some powered sites have paths and if you have a back door it’s not long enough.Holes in grass area .Asked to have fire place lit by office staff and told no.We replied but it’s winter.The answer was still no.Why have a fire place if u don’t use it???Pool area was very good

Run down and needing a big refreshment. The photos I saw on the site must have been of the newer ones, as the one I had felt like it had been here for 30 years and was needing major upgrading. Ok the positives, great friendly staff, great facilities on site. But the cabin I was in, 1990 here I come ! old worn tired   , holes in curtains, the bathroom didn't have a nice smell, (it actually smelt like a public urnial, I had to keep the door shut at all times...not good at all, the rooms don't have washing machines, dryers, and dishwashers, and its over 200 bucks a night ???  It feels like an old caravan , for the price I expected better.  Unless you get a new ones I would ask to make sure you cabin is NEW.

We had a very nice stay at a very LOW peak time (the week before the school holidays in July).  The park was nearly empty!  The kids loved the pool and the facilities were very clean. We had a powered en-suite site at the very rear of the park where literally no-one was around us. I was throwing the ball to our little dog and was told off (very rudely) by park staff who didn't initially identify himself. I thought he was just some disgruntled guest who had nothing better to do.  I understand that pets need to be on a lead at all times but NO ONE WAS AROUND!  I mean, come on!  Other than this, we had a pleasant enough stay.  Location is nowhere near the beach but very close to the shops (such as Woolies, etc.) which we could walk to.
Fantastic park, shout out to Michelle the park manager for making us welcome and the stories told around the communal fire
Michelle is a wonderful manager
Had a lovely night staying at this holiday park

Great caravan park. Lots of kid friendly activities including bounce balloon, golf course and even a heated pool! Well located to be able to walk into town easily.
Only minor negative is that the playground is not open, which was disappointing for our 3 year old.
Great caravan park we had a ensuite site  which was great and clean and tidy. The host were friendly and helpful , we will be back

Stayed here and it was great, however the staff were rude, and they put up their prices by 150% for the following year making it by far the dearest in town and not near the beach. You can stay for half the price in the centre of Sydney instead of here! Staying at a better park next time for 1/3 of the price by the beach and newer facilities. I don't normally post bad reviews. Please thumbs up if helpful
Awesome caravan Park very friendly staff check in was a breeze en suite very clean grounds well kept well worth the 6 hour drive

Stayed here last week for 2 nights in the new holiday units. So pleasant and quiet we returned the following week for 3 nights. The staff are terrific and show much pride in their maintaining and care of the park. The cleaners do an acceptional job with the rooms. My husband  desperately needed to relax and loved the undercover heated pool and hot spa with jets. We spent every possible moment in that pool, it's crystal clean and is heated as they claim. Love the place and will be staying here again.

All sites relatively flat and well grassed and mowed. Check- in was quick even with a last minute change on our part. Staff were helpful and friendly. Location really close to supermarket and clubs was perfect and park was quiet and relaxed. Facilities really good for families. Adjacent creek with lizards and birds a bonus. Highly recommend this park.

Tasman Holiday Park - nach einigen Versuchen entschieden wir uns für den Platz 48 an der Rückseite einer Kabine, um etwas Wind- und Sonnenschutz zu haben. Das Gelände für Camper ist ausreichend dimensioniert; die Camperküche bietet viel Platz und Musik (das gilt auch für die Sanitärräume). Wir hatten einen sehr angenehmen Aufenthalt hier und wurden am Morgen von Kaninchen, Enten und einem Purpurhuhn begrüßt.

It's better to through your money in the bin. We have waited for more then 2 hours, but then also we didn't got our keys. After a drive of more then 3 hours and with kids, it was really difficult for me.

We had the best stay here this Christmas holidays, our family rented two holiday units and we stayed in our caravan on a powered site. It has a really good atmosphere here, the weather wasn’t the greatest but they have an indoor heated pool and spa which was great. Staff were friendly, helpful and generous.
The rooms and facilities were so clean, also the grounds were neat. You can tell everyone takes pride in their work.
Few minutes walk to supermarket and CBD. We will be back here again!
Beautiful park. Great location.  Good for kids. Would definetely recommend and be back

It was our first time at Sapphire Valley and we had a great time. The park is pretty quiet and super family oriented.

We had a powered site with a concrete slab, it was great!

The pool is heated, indoor and a pretty decent size. This was fantastic, especially on the rainy days. Plus there was heaps of things for the kids to do. Jumping pillow, go carts and beach volleyball to name a few.

The amenities were quite modern and always clean.

The park is walking distance to the local Aldi and Woolworths supermarkets. Walk an extra 10min and your in the heart of Merimbula or 5 min by car. Either way, location is great.

Highly recommended this park for families.
Misleading information in advertising, very small Rooms and dirty. Lack of room amenities and facilities.
Very neat & clean park. Awesome cabins, very tidy.
Spent a few nights in a tent! Was awesome! Camp kitchen has everything you'd need and more.

We stayed in the dog friendly cabin and it was exceptional.
Large balcony/porch with bbq and locked gate so dogs can roam free.
The cabins back onto the mountain view of kangaroos.
The park has great facilities
Indoor heated pool, games room.
Large nature surrounding.
Staff are friendly
It has everything you need.
And good pricing
Will definitely stay again
Great spot for the kids plenty to do walking distance to town
Very accomodating, great service! Looking forward to stay there

Stayed in a holiday unit. Modern style room with everything you need. Comfy bed. Excellent facilities, great for families. Walking distance to town.
Unit just needs a more of a thorough clean:
- dirty sock under bed
- need to move beds away from wall & vacuum behind, as there was lots of dust, hair & bits of rubbish
- Food crumbs on chair cushions
- hair on glass shower screen
- Curtains are dirty/mouldy
- Need to remove light cover & clean as covered in bugs
- Need to remove exhaust fan cover in bathroom as it’s dirty and dusty
Very clean and modern. Great camp kitchen and swimming pool. Close to the town 10 minutes.

Great place to stay, very well maintained with impeccably clean facilities.  Close walking distance to shops, lots of wildlife in park. Friendly helpful staff.  Kangaroo poop is a bit annoying but rakes supplied next to bin areas. Highly recommended
Have been going here for 15+ years but now it has become over priced and simply unaffordable with 3 kids for what features the park has.
Fantastic holiday park. Pool is actually warm enough to swim in all year round. This is our kid’s favourite caravan park. Great facilities and friendly staff.
Great cabin, bathroom small but functional.  Mattresses very thin was only concern.
If you are staying at Merimbula  caravan or booking a cabin look no further sapphire Valley caravan Park it ticks all the boxes first class facilities and amenities all in the one place.

This is our first ever van holiday tasman caravan park Merimbula is flasher than Michael Jackson super friendly staff who went out of there way with a little issue we had, very clean amenities we will definitely return
My family had an amazing time here. The staff are very accommodating and friendly.

We stayed on a powered site, love this park and will definitely be back.
So many activities for kids and the amenities are spotless.
The grounds are amazing, loved hear the bell birds and Kookaburra's and watching the kangaroos.
Best place to stay, especially for kids, so many activities to keep them entertained. Definitely will stay again!
Great location, really good service, spacious and peaceful

Very helpful staff!
Clean and modern accommodation
Staff very friendly.  Very relaxing time away. Definitely recommend.  Also dog friendly cabins.

We had a fabulous week staying on an En-suite site up the back of the park so you feel like you are in a bush setting. The park grounds are immaculately maintained the guys do an awesome job. The ladies in reception were super friendly & helpful even suggesting places to check out while we were there great recommendations 👍🏼 Toasty warm indoor heated pool & spa to enjoy + mini golf. Plenty of other activities for those with kids. Easy walk to town and the beach.  Great value would definitely stay again 👍🏼
Wonderful stay at this park with the spa centre. Walking distance to shops, cafes and restaurants. Wonderful area to get fresh local seafood.

The lady that checked me in was amazing and so friendly. The man who cooked the complimentary pancakes was super dooper! Excellent park and fascilities. Can't find anything to fault. Have given a 4 purely becausr upon on checking out the darker haired lady was not so friendly. I would still recommend this park over NRMA Merimbula. I think the fascilities are better. Attitude is changeable! (Hopefully 😁)
Great facilities
Clean, comfortable quiet and affordable, nice staff
